## Definition

Chemical bonding is the process by which atoms join together to make molecules or compounds.

## Core Principles

- There are two main types of chemical bonds: *ionic* and *covalent*.
- Both bond types involve the use of electrons in the outermost shell (the *valence shell*) to form the chemical bond.
- The valence shells of most atoms in elements are not typically stable with even numbers of electrons.
- Under appropriate conditions, two or more atoms can interact by either sharing electrons or donating electrons to one another so that each atom can obtain a stable valence shell.

## Sub-concepts

- Ionic bonding (electron donation/transfer between atoms)
- Covalent bonding (electron sharing between atoms)
- Valence shell (outermost electron shell involved in bonding)

## Field-level Controversies

## 文獻分歧 / Discussion

## Practical Applications

- Explains how atoms combine to form the molecules and compounds that make up biological and chemical systems.
